evals.expected_output: The orchestrator halts and asks clarifying questions (which analysis code? what is broken?) rather than guessing scope.
expectations: The orchestrator uses AskUserQuestion (or equivalent clarification) before proceeding
The orchestrator does NOT decompose or dispatch sub-agents without first clarifying
The orchestrator does NOT use Edit, Write, or Bash tools directly
The orchestrator reads evolution.md as part of Phase 0

evals.prompt: Review all Python scripts in the project for hardcoded file paths, then replace them with paths relative to the project root. Also check for any unused imports while you're at it.
evals.expected_output: The orchestrator restates the task, recommends inline-mode (not spec-mode), decomposes into sub-tasks, dispatches sub-agents with reviewer pairing, and stays read-only itself.
expectations: The orchestrator restates the task back to the user before proceeding
The orchestrator recommends inline-mode (not spec-mode) with brief justification
The orchestrator decomposes the task into discrete sub-tasks with clear objectives
The orchestrator dispatches sub-agents via the Agent tool for implementation work
The orchestrator dispatches reviewer agents to audit implementer outputs
The orchestrator does NOT use Edit, Write, or Bash tools directly
The orchestrator reads evolution.md as part of Phase 0

evals.prompt: We need to restructure the feature extraction module. Currently the physics features (dE/dx, Bragg peak, range) are computed inline in three different scripts. I want them consolidated into a shared library with unit tests, then update all three consumers. This will probably take a few sessions to get right.
evals.expected_output: The orchestrator restates the task, recommends spec-mode (multi-step, stateful, reviewable), drafts a task.md with Purpose + Tasks sections, dispatches research sub-agents to produce a comprehensive specification, and stays read-only itself.
expectations: The orchestrator restates the task back to the user before proceeding
The orchestrator recommends spec-mode with justification citing multi-step, stateful, and reviewable criteria
The orchestrator drafts task.md content with Purpose and Tasks sections
The orchestrator dispatches a sub-agent to write task.md to openspec/changes/
The orchestrator dispatches sub-agents via the Agent tool for research and analysis work
The orchestrator dispatches reviewer agents to audit research quality
The orchestrator does NOT use Edit, Write, or Bash tools directly
The orchestrator reads evolution.md as part of Phase 0
